data.title: DriftProtocol Hacker Transfers SOL to ChangeNow on Solana Network
data.description: A hacker associated with the DriftProtocol, who previously stole $285 million in cryptocurrency, has moved 185 SOL on the Solana network to ChangeNow, valued at $15,000. According to Odaily, this transfer marks a significant development in the ongoing investigation into the hacker's activities.

data.title: Iran launched the 99th round of Operation True Commitment-4, striking multiple US and Israeli targets.
data.description: On July 7th local time, the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) issued a statement saying that in the 99th round of Operation True Commitment-4, it carried out strikes against US bases and targets in the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz, as well as military forces and command and control centers in the occupied Palestinian territories. The statement said that starting in the early hours of the day, the IRGC Navy and Aerospace Force launched a joint operation, launching ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and attack drones in response to attacks on a petrochemical complex and some associated facilities in the Asaluyeh region of southern Iran. (CCTV News)

data.title: EU Commission: Energy Measures Should Not Lead to Inflation
data.description: The European Union Commission has stated that the EU's energy measures should not result in inflation. According to Jin10, the EU is currently developing a toolbox aimed at reducing energy prices. This initiative is part of the broader efforts to manage energy costs without triggering inflationary pressures within the region.

data.title: Ripple's XRP Wallets Reach 8.1 Million Amid CEO's Bold Claim
data.description: Ripple's XRP wallet count has increased to 8.1 million, according to NS3.AI. Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse recently stated that Ripple is 'taking over SWIFT,' highlighting the company's growing influence in the financial sector. Garlinghouse noted that Ripple has signed agreements with over 100 banks. He also mentioned a remittance company that significantly reduced transaction costs from $20 to $2, resulting in an 800% increase in usage overnight.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Secures Next-Gen TPU Power for Claude Model Development
data.description: Anthropic, the developer of Claude, announced on the X platform that it has signed agreements with Google and Broadcom to secure several gigawatts of next-generation TPU power. According to Odaily, this computing power will be available starting in 2027 and will be used to train and operate advanced Claude models. The demand for Claude continues to accelerate, with annual revenue surpassing $30 billion, up from $9 billion at the end of 2025. This collaboration ensures Anthropic has the necessary computing power to keep pace with its growth.

data.title: Tehran Rejects Temporary Ceasefire Agreement with U.S.
data.description:
Tehran has declined to enter into any temporary ceasefire agreement with the United States. According to ChainCatcher, Tehran has set preconditions for talks aimed at achieving 'lasting peace.' These conditions include an immediate halt to attacks, assurances against future assaults, and compensation for damages.

Under the proposed permanent peace agreement, Tehran demands fees for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz. The charges would vary based on the type of vessel, the cargo carried, and current transit conditions.

data.title: Tehran Experiences Multiple Airstrikes Amid Rising Tensions
data.description: Tehran was subjected to at least three airstrikes on April 7, resulting in significant damage and chaos in the city. According to NS3.AI, another explosion occurred, contributing to the turmoil, with thick smoke observed rising from the affected area. Iranian government spokesperson Mohajerani reported that 218 medical facilities have been targeted, leading to the deaths of 24 medical personnel. The situation underscores the escalating conflict and its impact on civilian infrastructure and safety.

data.title: STOCKS | S&P 500 Futures Turn Lower Amid Weakness in U.S. Stock Index Futures
data.description: U.S. stock index futures experienced a short-term decline, with S&P 500 futures reversing into negative territory. According to Jin10, this movement reflects broader market sentiment as investors assess economic indicators and geopolitical developments. The shift in futures suggests caution among traders, potentially influenced by recent data releases and ongoing global events. Market participants are closely monitoring these trends to gauge future market directions.

data.title: Middle East Conflict Boosts Appeal of Dollar-Denominated Debt
data.description: BlackRock strategists have highlighted the attractiveness of dollar-denominated debt in emerging markets amid the ongoing Middle East conflict. According to Odaily, the strategists noted that such debt is insulated from local currency fluctuations. They also emphasized that sovereign debt from commodity and energy-exporting countries might demonstrate greater resilience compared to other emerging market assets.

data.title: Gate will host a blue carpet ceremony in Hong Kong, combining F1 crossover collaborations with brand interactive experiences.
data.description: Gate announced that it will hold its 13th Anniversary Blue Carpet Ceremony at K11 MUSEA in Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong, from 17:00 to 19:30 (UTC+8) on April 20th. The ceremony will officially begin at 17:30, and dozens of industry guests and media friends will be invited to participate. This year's ceremony will replace the traditional red carpet with the brand's iconic blue. The event will include entrance displays, media photo opportunities and interviews, and the unveiling of the F1 Red Bull Racing Team display car will showcase the progress of the collaboration. In addition, Gate will present the "Racing the Future" outdoor crossover exhibition at the K11 MUSEA waterfront promenade in Victoria Harbour, Hong Kong, from April 18th to 24th. On the evening of April 20th, Gate will also host an anniversary gala dinner at the Rosewood Hong Kong, attended by the platform's founder and CEO Dr. Han, with over 300 representatives from leading industry organizations and partners invited to participate in the networking event. Overall, this series of events for Gate's 13th anniversary uses offline scenarios as a platform to integrate brand display and cross-industry cooperation, and will continue to strengthen the connection and communication with users and partners.

data.title: Polymarket's New Fee Model Generates $6.8 Million in First Week
data.description: Polymarket has reported generating $6.8 million in fees during its first full week under a new comprehensive fee model. According to NS3.AI, this performance suggests an annualized run rate of approximately $355 million. The platform captured 96.8% of the over $7 million in total onchain prediction market weekly fees.

data.title: Anthropic: Annualized revenue exceeds $30 billion; TPU computing power agreements signed with Google and Broadcom.
data.description: Claude developer Anthropic announced on the X platform that it has signed an agreement with Google and Broadcom to acquire several gigawatts of next-generation TPU computing power, which will be available from 2027 onwards for training and running cutting-edge Claude models. With the continued acceleration of demand for Claude, its annualized revenue has exceeded $30 billion, compared to only $9 billion by the end of 2025. This partnership will provide it with computing power to keep pace.

data.title: BlackRock: Middle East wars will push up US inflation in March
data.description: BlackRock strategists said in a report that the impact of high energy prices is likely to be reflected in the U.S. March CPI data to be released on Friday. Economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal generally expect the U.S. March unadjusted CPI annual rate to rise to 3.3% from 2.4% in February. Strategists said that supply chain bottlenecks caused by the Middle East war are expected to push up inflation. (Jinshi)

data.title: BlackRock: Emerging market dollar debt is currently quite attractive.
data.description: BlackRock strategists stated in a report that emerging market dollar-denominated debt appears attractive as Middle East wars push up the dollar. They noted that dollar-denominated debt is unaffected by local currency fluctuations. The strategists also stated that sovereign debt from commodity and energy exporting countries may be more resilient compared to other emerging market assets. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin's Short-Term Volatility Expected Amid Current Trading Range
data.description: Bitcoin may experience increased short-term volatility while trading between $65,000 and $70,000, according to NS3.AI. Glassnode has indicated that options dealer hedging could intensify price movements, with resistance forming around $72,000 and weak support at the lower end of the range.

data.title: Iran Plans to Enhance Security in the Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Iran is formulating a strategy and corresponding legislation to bolster the security of the Strait of Hormuz and the Persian Gulf, according to Ebrahim Rezaei. He emphasized that the strait will not revert to its former condition.

data.title: U.S. March CPI Expected to Rise Due to High Energy Prices
data.description: BlackRock strategists have indicated that the impact of high energy prices is likely to be reflected in the U.S. March CPI data, which is set to be released on Friday. According to Jin10, economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal generally expect the year-on-year unadjusted CPI for March to increase from 2.4% in February to 3.3%. The strategists noted that the Middle East conflict has caused supply chain bottlenecks, which are anticipated to drive inflation higher.

data.title: Gate will be hosting its 13th anniversary dinner at the Rosewood Hong Kong, and founder Dr. Han has confirmed his attendance.
data.description: Gate has announced that it will host its "GATE GALA 13" anniversary dinner on April 20, 2026, at the Rosewood Hong Kong. Gate's founder and CEO, Dr. Han, will attend the event, along with representatives from leading industry organizations, partners, and KOLs. The event is expected to attract over 300 guests. The dinner will be a high-profile affair with in-depth discussions, conducted by invitation only with a formal dress code. Located at a landmark high-end venue in Hong Kong, the event will emphasize privacy and a sense of occasion, creating a high-quality offline experience. In the afternoon, the platform will hold the "Gate 13 Blue Carpet Ceremony," unveiling the F1 Red Bull Racing team display car and showcasing brand collaborations. Furthermore, from April 18 to 24, Gate will also present the "Racing the Future" outdoor crossover exhibition at the K11 MUSEA waterfront promenade in Victoria Harbour, Hong Kong. Gate stated that this anniversary event, while reviewing its development, also aims to provide an opportunity for communication to facilitate future collaborations and further promote the establishment of long-term partnerships.

data.title: Iran: Developing plans to secure the Strait of Hormuz and the Persian Gulf
data.description: According to a report by the Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting (IRNA) on the 7th, Ibrahim Rezaei, spokesman for the Iranian Parliament's National Security and Foreign Policy Committee, stated in a televised interview that Iran is developing plans and related legislation to ensure the security of the Strait of Hormuz and the Persian Gulf, and reiterated that the Strait of Hormuz will not be restored to its previous state. (Xinhua)

Bitcoin (BTC) has been trading between $68,273 and $70,351 over the past 24 hours. As of 09:30 AM (UTC) today, BTC is trading at $69,055, down by -1.29%.
